title: Aluminum Drops as Trump Moves to Narrow Levies on Metal Products
summary: Aluminum prices fell after reports that President Donald Trump planned to roll back some levies on aluminum and steel products, easing an earlier market rally tied to tariffs. The clip says aluminum dropped as much as 2.7% on the London Metal Exchange amid a broader selloff in industrial metals.

title: US to build largest aluminum plant with 500,000-ton annual capacity
summary: The article reports that the United States is preparing to build its first new aluminum smelter since the 1980s, with a planned annual capacity of 500,000 tons. It says the project, led by Century Aluminum in Inola, Oklahoma, is intended to reduce dependence on imported aluminum and includes 50,000 tons of high-purity metal for defense uses.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers reported a nanofiltration process designed to recover aluminum ions from manufacturing waste streams. The team said the membrane captured more than 99 percent of aluminum ions in lab tests, which could raise yield and reduce hazardous waste from aluminum production.

title: 3 technologies helping the aluminum industry decarbonize
summary: This article highlights three technology pathways aimed at reducing emissions in aluminum production, including decarbonizing smelting, improving recycled aluminum quality, and using hydrogen in alumina refining. It emphasizes sustainability gains from both process innovation and cleaner energy inputs.

title: 3 technologies helping the aluminium industry decarbonize
summary: The World Economic Forum article describes three innovations that can help lower emissions in aluminum production. It points to closed-loop smelting technology, purification of recycled aluminum, and hydrogen use in refining as important decarbonization tools.

title: HRC Steel rises to highest level since December 2023 as prices continue climbing
summary: Hot-rolled coil steel increased to 1,162.02 USD/T on May 26, 2026, up 0.34% from the previous day. The broader market trend cited on the page shows continued strength in steel prices over recent weeks and months.

title: Steel price news: prices continue to move higher across flat, plate, and long products
summary: Ryerson reports that steel prices are still moving higher across flat, plate, and long products. The article attributes the trend to constrained supply and improving demand, which are reinforcing the price increase.

title: Steel price falls in Trading Economics market snapshot

title: US domestic steel prices continue to rise amid tight supply
summary: Steel Market Update says domestic steel prices in the US continued to increase for most products. The publication links the rise to resilient demand and tight supply, with sheet and plate prices moving higher.

title: Current State of the U.S. Steel Market
summary: The article says U.S. steel market conditions showed signs of stabilization, with production for the week of May 10, 2025 running above both the year-to-date average and the same week a year earlier. It also notes mill capability utilization rose to 77.5%, though capacity utilization remained below the administration’s 80% goal.

title: Community reacts to news of $2.7 billion dollar steel sheet mill
summary: This segment discusses Nucor’s announced $2.7 billion steel sheet mill in Apple Grove, Mason County. It says the facility is expected to create jobs and produce about 3 million tons of steel annually once operational.

title: Latest developments in steelmaking capacity and outlook until 2026
summary: The report says global steelmaking capacity reached 2,432 million tonnes in 2023, far exceeding global steel production and demand. It highlights persistent excess capacity and provides an outlook through 2026.

title: Industry Data
summary: AISI reports that U.S. domestic raw steel production in the week ending May 16, 2026 was 1,898,000 net tons. The same update puts capability utilization at 82.2%, indicating relatively strong current operating rates.

title: U.S. Steel Manufacturers Plan Near-Term Capacity Additions
summary: This article says U.S. steel manufacturers are planning more than $6 billion in capital-spending projects. The projects are described as near-term capacity additions that are expected to begin in the second half of the year.

title: 3 innovations in green steel
summary: This article highlights several steel-sector sustainability innovations, including Boston Metal’s CO2-free steel process, Kobe Steel’s lower-coke blast furnace technique, and hydrogen-based steelmaking. It also describes H2 Green Steel’s plan to build a large commercial hydrogen steel plant aimed at producing green steel at scale.

title: The Future of Green Steel: Reducing Carbon Emissions in Metallurgy
summary: The article explains how hydrogen-based direct reduction of iron can dramatically cut emissions in steel production. It also emphasizes the role of renewable-powered green hydrogen and policy support in accelerating adoption of low-carbon metallurgy.

title: Recent Significant Innovations in the Steel Industry
summary: This piece surveys a range of steel-industry innovations, including green hydrogen, carbon capture and storage, AI-driven manufacturing, 3D printing, electrification, and circular economy practices. title: Iron and Steel Technology Roadmap

title: The Steel Industry in 2024: 10 Things To Remember
summary: The article notes that the steel industry is increasingly adopting greener methods to reduce carbon emissions and environmental impact. It discusses sustainability-related trends as part of the sector’s broader transformation in 2024.

title: Copper up 9% since Iran war, near January peak. Will AI boom, shortage propel red metal to new highs?

title: Capstone Copper Reports Record Fourth Quarter 2025 Results

title: Copper must scale up production within sustainable limits

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Bloomberg reports that PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's biggest nickel mine in Indonesia, has been told to sharply reduce output as the country tries to support global nickel prices. The company said it plans to apply for a revision to the quota, which Bloomberg says would fall from 42 million tons in 2025 to 12 million tons this year.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: S&P Global reports that Indonesia confirmed plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue. The article says the government is also reversing parts of its mining quota approach to better regulate supply volatility and curb environmentally harmful operations.

title: Global Nickel Market
summary: This market update says the IEA estimates global mine nickel supply rose from 3.6 million metric tons in 2023 to 3.9 million metric tons in 2024, nearly a 10% increase. It also notes that the market surplus in 2024 was about 150,000 tons, suggesting the market is relatively tight despite the supply increase.

title: Trading Economics Nickel Price News
summary: Trading Economics reports that nickel futures rose back above $18,800 per ton after hitting a near four-week low on May 18. The move was attributed to reports of additional output reductions, reflecting market sensitivity to supply-cut headlines.

title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future

title: Q&A: Experts argue clean energy must secure a sustainable supply of nickel

title: Green nickel for sustainable electrification
